research_series.series_name: Evidence Grading,Source Quality,and Graph Edge Promotion Rules

patterns.pattern_name: Graph edge promotion requires structured evidence grading and risk-of-bias assessment

mediator.name: evidence_strength_and_risk_of_bias
mediator.pathway: source quality, risk of bias, consistency, directness, precision and publication bias determine whether an edge can be promoted
mediator.mechanism: structured evidence grading prevents promotion of weak associations to causal graph edges

inference_rule.plain_language_rule: Promote edges only when replicated, direct, human evidence supports the relation; keep mechanistic-only or single-study findings as hypothesis nodes.

hypotheses.reason: GRADE/ROBINS frameworks require explicit assessment of bias, inconsistency, indirectness, imprecision and publication bias.

evidence.summary: GRADE and ROBINS-style tools provide structured evidence assessment. Knowledge graph edge promotion should require replicated human/intervention or strong chain-of-causality support, not statistical association alone.

key_sources.title: Evidence Grading, Source Quality, Risk of Bias, and Graph Edge Promotion Rules in Biomedical Research
key_sources.relevant_finding: GRADE is robust; ROBINS tools help assess bias; graph edge promotion requires replicated human/intervention evidence, not just association.
key_sources.limitations: Direct graph edge promotion literature remains sparse.

limitations: Knowledge graph-specific edge promotion rules are underdeveloped.
Risk-of-bias tools are complex and can be misapplied.
alternative_explanations: High-quality mechanistic evidence may support a hypothesis but not promotion to causal edge without human support.

negative_or_uncertain_findings.claim: One statistically significant small study is sufficient to promote a graph edge.
negative_or_uncertain_findings.reason: Evidence grading frameworks require replication, directness, risk-of-bias evaluation and effect-size interpretation.

high_value_candidate_nodes.definition: An edge has enough replicated, direct and low-bias evidence to be used as a stable inference relation.
Structured assessment of systematic error risk in the evidence supporting a node or edge.

research_gaps: Formal graph edge promotion standards for biomedical inference graphs.

most_supported_patterns: Risk-of-bias assessment is essential before edge promotion.
highest_value_patterns_for_inference: Separate evidence strength, edge confidence, causal status and personal applicability.
patterns_requiring_caution: AI/LLM risk-of-bias assessment is promising but not ready to replace expert review.
recommended_next_queries: Design BodyState Mapper edge promotion rules from GRADE/ROBINS concepts.

works_cited.title: Evidence Grading, Source Quality, Risk of Bias, and Graph Edge Promotion Rules in Biomedical Research
